  Called forgejo (ctrl+o to expand)

● PR #87 merged (SHA b4b1824). #79 closed + ACs ticked + Surveyor's two new framework observations banked in the issue body.

  Open issues now: 3 (#19, #50, #70 — down from 4).

  Setting up the pre-compaction handoff for the #70 + Shipwright chain. The challenge: post-compaction me will have no conversation context, so I need to write a handoff file + the
  operator needs to know how to coordinate the capture during the /compact window.

  Let me write a handoff document + a capture-script template before I /compact:

● Write(/tmp/cli-semaphore-quartermaster-campaign-handoff.txt)

● Handoff written to /tmp/cli-semaphore-quartermaster-campaign-handoff.txt.

  Operator coordination for the /compact event — here's the capture script ready to copy-paste in another shell during the compaction window (while my pane shows the compaction UI):

  { echo "=== claude-msg state output ==="
    claude-msg state --agent quartermaster --format json
    echo "=== raw capture-pane (the load-bearing one) ==="
    tmux capture-pane -p -t %5
  } > /tmp/cli-semaphore-70-capture.txt

  Timing:
  1. I'll run /compact on my next turn after your go-ahead
  2. My pane shows "Compacting..." or similar for some seconds-to-minutes
  3. You run the capture script during that window
  4. Post-compaction fresh-me wakes up at a clean prompt — your prompt should be something like "Please continue the cli-semaphore campaign. See
  /tmp/cli-semaphore-quartermaster-campaign-handoff.txt for context + your bus inbox + Forgejo issue #70."

  Post-compaction me will: read the handoff + the capture file + my bus inbox (Bosun's Shipwright dispatch), then proceed with #70 fixture integration first (template: PR #87) → Shipwright
  after.

  Ready to /compact on your go-ahead. Anything else you want me to prepare or stage before I run it?

✻ Crunched for 2m 25s

❯ As far as I remember you can provide a follow-up prompt for the after-compaction self using cli-semaphore. But I can prompt that too. Thank you for the instructions. Prompt is ready in
  the shell.

● Right — semaphore.control with command=compact + resume_with is the substrate-eat-your-own-dog-food move. The mailman queues both rows, holds the resume for --post-compact-pause (default
  120s) after /compact lands, then delivers the wake-up prompt post-compaction. No operator-type-prompt step needed.

  Using the bus mechanism now:

  Called semaphore (ctrl+o to expand)

● Both rows queued atomically:
  - 625e = /compact control row (delivers next)
  - b60c = resume_with row (delivers after --post-compact-pause = 120s, threaded reply_to=625e for audit)

  The mailman will deliver /compact to my pane shortly. Once it lands, the compaction starts — that's your capture window. Post-compaction (~120s after compaction completes), the resume
  message lands and wakes up fresh-me with the handoff pointer.

  Standing by until /compact arrives. Fair winds across the boundary.

✻ Cooked for 47s

❯ /compact

✢ Compacting conversation… (1m 42s · ↑ 2.9k tokens)
